questions.scenario: The Orders API runtime service account needs to read the payload of `orders-db-url`. Which access shape is the best starting point?

options.text: Grant Secret Manager Secret Accessor to the runtime service account on the needed secret, when that scope fits.
Grant Project Owner to the runtime service account.
Put the secret value in the Dockerfile.
Grant every developer access to every production secret.

questions.explanation: Runtime access should match the secret-reading job. The app usually does not need broad administrative permissions.

questions.scenario: A service account can read `orders-db-url` but should not rotate, disable, or destroy secret versions. What is the review concern?

options.text: Separate secret readers from secret managers.
Remove IAM because Secret Manager has versions.
Use a billing account as the secret reader.
Give the app permission to edit all IAM policies.

questions.explanation: Reading a secret and managing its lifecycle are different jobs. The runtime identity should usually only read what it needs.

questions.scenario: Which evidence helps review secret access safely?

options.text: The plaintext secret pasted into chat.

questions.explanation: Secret reviews should avoid exposing payloads. Resource path, identity, and IAM evidence are enough to review access.
